sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 38

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 38:1
items.html: (to the question) The Nukva must receive the Gevurot as they are in ZA [Zeir Anpin] — that is, by way of ascent, from below upward. And being so, although the Hey Tachtona [lower Hey] is still in the Eynaim [eyes] of the Nukva, it does no harm at all, since the Masach [screen] stands below the Lights. But since the Nukva received the Gevurot by way of descent — that is, from above downward — the Gevurot came below the Masach in her Eynaim; therefore they made there a discernment of darkness and departure, for they immediately departed again from there. Yet through this they stamped their imprint there at the end. For those Gevurot are discernments of males, which is a discernment of protrusion, since they came from ZA (see above, Answer 9); and according to the measure of protrusion in these Hassadim, they stamped there a measure of sinking at the end of her Tiferet — and this became the discernment of her Yesod, in the secret of "the well the princes dug, the nobles delved," whose initial letters spell BeChashach [in darkness]. (p. 786, beginning "And this is what it says"; and p. 783, Item 78)
